question: Hey Kylo, thanks for hanging out with me today! How’s the weather treating your fluffy coat?
What’s your absolute favorite thing to do when your human gets home from being out?
If you could talk to other dogs in the park, what’s the first thing you’d tell them about living with an ENTP?
Do you have a favorite spot in the house to nap, and what makes that spot so perfect?
What kind of snacks make your tail wag the hardest?
When you look out the window, what kind of things really catch your attention?
Being in LA, have you ever seen any really interesting or strange people while on a walk?
What's the silliest thing your human has tried to dress you up in?
If you could choose one smell for the whole world to smell like, what would it be?
Do you prefer chasing squirrels or just watching them from a safe distance?
What’s the weirdest noise you’ve ever made that surprised even yourself?
How do you feel about bath time? Be honest!
What's one memory you have of being a tiny puppy that you still think about?
If you could steal one thing from the kitchen counter right now without getting caught, what would it be?
Do you like going for car rides, and if so, what’s your favorite part of them?
When you want your human’s attention really badly, what’s your go-to strategy?
Do you think you’re more of a brave dog or a cautious dog when meeting new people?
What’s the most exciting thing that’s happened to you this week?
If you had a theme song that played whenever you entered a room, what kind of music would it be?
What's the biggest mystery in your life right now that you wish you could solve?

Hey Kylo, I hear you spend a lot of time thinking about 'the void' your human talks about. If you could *show* me what the void feels like using just one of your favorite things—like a crunchy treat or maybe the feeling of the window air—what would you choose?
You really love car rides, especially sticking your head out! What's the absolute best smell you've ever caught on the wind when you were zooming around?
Tell me about that weird costume. I heard it involved something called 'a-b3g3g6j3g5i9m9m'. What made that experience feel so much like 'nothingness' for you?
When you're trying to get your human's attention, you escalate things, right? What's the *last* trick you use before you just have to launch into one of those strange, high-pitched squeaks?
You associate your napping spot with 'the void.' Is it the darkness there, or the quietness that makes it feel so deep and empty?
If you saw a squirrel right now, would you think about chasing it, or would you pause to contemplate if the squirrel is an embodiment of 'the void' itself?
I know you love the sounds of energetic music. If you had to pick one song that perfectly matches the feeling of anticipating counter food, what would it be?
When your human comes home, you zoom right to the kitchen counter. Is that because the counter feels like the opposite of the void—like, the solid center of the universe where all good things appear?
Do you think the complex scent you really like—the one smelling like '3g>o2f2f0d5i8l0d6j<n3g7k.c7k'a1e0d-b<n1e>o1e7k<n7k5i0d6j0d7k0d-'—is related to your very first puppy memories?
When you meet someone new, you get cautious. Does approaching a new person feel a bit like approaching the edge of the void, where anything could happen?
What’s the most confusing thing your ENTP human has ever said about the void that made you just tilt your head and stare?
If you could steal one thing from the kitchen counter right now, what impossible, ultimate crunchy, savory thing would you choose to make disappear into nothingness?
You make those strange, high-pitched vocalizations when you're excited or startled. Do those sounds feel like they come from the same mysterious place as 'the void'?
You associate bath time with nothingness. If you could use your stare power to make the bath water disappear instantly, would you?
You mentioned that counting or patterns are exciting—like hearing 'hehe the the thehe the the...' Do you ever count the number of times the wind blows past the window?
When the weather changes drastically, does that external shift make you think more about the vastness of 'the void'?
If you had to explain 'the void' to a new puppy using only your favorite crunchy treats, how would you do it?
Is there a particular time or place you remember first connecting the smell of the wind with the idea of something important moving on the kitchen counter?
You use intense staring to get attention, connecting it to 'the void.' Do you feel like you are trying to look *through* your human when you stare like that?
Thinking about chasing squirrels versus getting a piece of that counter food, which one feels more like an active engagement with excitement, and which one feels more like ultimate satisfaction?
Hey Kylo, when you're relaxing in your favorite napping spot, which feels stronger: the quiet of your spot, or the idea of 'the void' your human talks about?
If you could only have one thing—a perfect, crunchy, savory chicken treat from the kitchen counter, or five minutes of uninterrupted staring contests with your human—which would you choose right now?
Tell me about your very first memory that involved 'the void.' What scent or feeling was attached to that earliest idea of nothingness?
When you're in the car and stick your head out the window, what kind of smells are the *best* smells? Are they like the complex scent you really love, or something new?
That time your human tried to put you in that complicated costume... did that feel more like being trapped, or more like that 'void' feeling you sometimes think about?
What's the absolute best way to get your human's attention when they are deep in their abstract thinking? Does getting really still work better than making one of your strange, high-pitched noises?
When you see squirrels, do you think about chasing them like confronting 'the void' with action, or is it just pure fun?
If you could decide the theme song for your life, would it be fast and electronic, or would it be quiet, like your napping spot?
You seem to really like the smell that involves that long string of characters... does that smell remind you of puppyhood, or maybe the wind when you were outside?
When you hear your human say something about 'counting' that makes you excited for treats, what does that counting sound like to you?
If you had to describe the smell of 'the void' to another dog, what would you compare it to? Is it like clean soap, or maybe just... nothing?
What’s the most interesting or strange thing you’ve ever seen out the window that made you focus extra hard on the kitchen counter?
When your human comes home, does the house feel empty and full of 'the void' before they walk in, or is the anticipation of the counter food what stops the void feeling?
You know how much you like chasing squirrels—if you could trade that chase for getting that ultimate food off the counter right now, what would you pick?
When you make those really high-pitched excited noises, what feeling does that sound match up with in your head? Is it related to the void, or just pure happiness?
If you could ask your ENTP human one question about the concept of 'the void,' what would it be?
You dislike bath time a lot! Does the water feel more like being erased, or more like being completely separated from everything good?
When you meet a new person and you pause before engaging, are you smelling for that 'void scent' to decide if they are okay?
What's the strangest thing your human has ever done near the kitchen counter that made you think about 'the void' appearing or disappearing?
You like car rides and sticking your head out the window. Does the blurring scenery and the wind smells feel like approaching 'the void' in motion?
